source: Classical Commercial Feng Shui, Modern Practice

rules.description: Executive desk must be in power/command position — back to wall, facing door
rules.notes: Never sit with back to door. Diagonal to the door is ideal. Solid wall behind represents mountain support.

rules.description: Desk should not directly face the door
rules.notes: Directly facing the door catches too much qi — creates confrontation energy. Offset slightly.

rules.description: Desk should not be under an exposed beam
rules.notes: Beam above the desk creates pressure, headaches, and blocked career progression.

rules.description: Desk should not face a wall directly (close range)
rules.notes: Facing a close wall blocks vision and career advancement. If unavoidable, hang landscape artwork showing depth.

rules.description: Desk should not be between two doors (qi corridor)
rules.notes: Sitting in the path between two doors creates instability and argumentative energy.

rules.description: CEO/owner office should be in the deepest part of the office, not near the entrance
rules.notes: The power position is furthest from the entrance. Reception handles the qi mouth; leadership commands from depth.

rules.description: Reception desk should face the main entrance
rules.notes: Receptionist is the gatekeeper of qi — should see everyone entering. Desk should not have back to the door.

rules.description: Avoid seating employees with backs to main circulation paths
rules.notes: Back to circulation = back to the door principle. Creates anxiety and reduces productivity.

rules.description: Accounting/finance department should be in the southeast sector
rules.notes: Southeast is the wealth sector. Placing financial operations here enhances money management.

rules.description: Store entrance should be wide and inviting — avoid narrow entries
rules.notes: Wider entrance gathers more qi (customers). Double doors preferred over single.

rules.description: Cash register should be in the diagonal corner from the entrance
rules.notes: The power corner (diagonal from qi entry) accumulates the most energy. Wealth collects here.

rules.description: Cash register should not directly face the front door
rules.notes: Money directly facing the door flows out. Position at an angle.

rules.description: Store layout should encourage clockwise circulation
rules.notes: Clockwise movement (entering and turning right) is natural and keeps customers in the store longer. Most retail layouts use this pattern.

rules.description: Avoid placing sale/discount items at the entrance
rules.notes: Cheap items at the qi mouth signal devaluation. Place premium items where qi first enters; sale items deeper inside.

rules.description: Restaurant kitchen fire (stove) should not be visible from the dining area entrance
rules.notes: Exposed fire creates anxiety for diners. Screen with partition or half-wall.

rules.description: Dining area should have bright, yang energy — well-lit with warm colors
rules.notes: Yang energy stimulates appetite and social interaction. Avoid dim, cave-like dining unless it's intentional ambiance.

rules.description: Cashier should not be placed near the toilet or kitchen waste area
rules.notes: Proximity to waste/drains at the point of payment implies wealth draining.
